item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remind me of Conway from Courage the Cowardly Dog.
Anyway, the articles is well written, though a four-man security team seem excessive. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I find this skip more funny than unsettling (he is a literal trashman…). The backstory kind of detracts from the humor that (I think) makes this article so good. +1 anyway

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last line in the interview…
AWWWW

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite its nonliving composition, SCP-2328 is animate, sentient

Shouldn't that be "sapient", not "sentient"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'm glad to be writing things for this site that people enjoy - even if their enjoyment is comparatively modest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So glad this is doing well, Mister-Smalls!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an excellent thought, Hawkin!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this reads a lot better now. Good job with the edits!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
when SCP-2328 was found, was he already packed up in suit and mask?

SCP-2328 is the suit and mask, as well as the refuse used to fill the suit out. The visible human skin is in fact part of the mask. It therefore seems unlikely that 2328 is Vershinski's biological son, or anyone else's for that matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought he'd come back. The trash always comes back.

And upvote. This is the kind of writing that makes humanoid skips work. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some changes based on feedback.

  • Cut the second interview (I agreed it was a weakness)
  • Added a little bit to hopefully clarify 2328's construct nature
  • Reduced the amount of trash produced per day from 20 cubic meters to 12
  • Made the picture smaller

Thanks for all the great feedback so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait. This is an Egregore of Hoarding.

From my POV, that is very very unsettling.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 3MB image. Please resize and reupload.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really interesting and detailed, and I was incredibly into it, but the first interview sort of bogged it down a bit. If the article ended at "Following these incidents, SCP-2328's initial discovery site was repurposed into a containment area for ease of containment.", I would have upvoted, but that second interview seems a bit cheap and not really justifying its spot there. So no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By this, he becomes cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else see some parallels to the Foundation in this scip? It presents a normal face that protects it from the outside world, but inside it is anything but "normal." It compulsively collects and contains hazardous materials that the general public doesn't want to handle (trash/anomalies). It gets upset when the things it collects are taken away, even if it is for the best. No questioning of authority is allowed.

And with that in mind…does the Foundation have a collective hoarding problem?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I may bring up something from the drafts forum, another user compared SCP-2328 to Pinocchio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's how I see it too. But IMO it should be made slightly clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr. ████: You threw him away?

SCP-2328: I thought he'd come back. The trash always comes back.

That's the best line in the interviews. It gives the greatest peek into SCP-2328's psyche.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
- is he really the son of Robert Vershinski?

I think it's his son in the same way Redd thinks of Dr.Wondertainment as his father. It's clearly not his biological father, but it's most likely its creator or at least its protector/tutor; thus assuming a parental role.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Interesting story!

Also, 20 cubic meters is a LOT of trash. I don't think one person could clean up that much trash in a day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the imagery; it's so surreal and bizarre. Hate the cheap attempt and emotional investment; saw it coming a mile away. Upvoting for now; may change upon further deliberation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually think it's a lot stronger without any indication of origin. Tooany scips try to explain to much. this is just a great big ball of fucking weird that they found.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An excellent example of 'WTF have I just read?'. In a good way, I hasten to add!

Fun facts:

  • when I saw the picture/read the 'mask attached to his face' part, I immediately thought: 'Where is my Mommy?' (Doctor Who, series 1 episode 9, The Empty Child) ;>
  • a few paragraphs later, I thought: 'is this an allegory for the cause of global warming?'

A few things that need to be clarified, IMO:
- when SCP-2328 was found, was he already packed up in suit and mask?
- is he really the son of Robert Vershinski? If he is, why is he described as a construct resembling a Caucasian male human? Also, why write with no known living relatives if Vershinski had a son?
I do believe SCP-2328 is not Vershinski's son, but… not clear enough. I think it would be good to mention somewhere, for instance, that the Foundation tried to get him out of his suit and had to give up, because he's not actually a man in a suit.

Still, all in all, a very good, original take on compulsive hoarding. Aaaaaand yes, upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: tentative upvote, but a hint as to this skip's origins would be nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I spent 2-3 minutes making up the image in my mind man. And all the amazing implications and twists I was able to think of. That alone deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Im torn. On one hand, the line "SCP-2328's visible facial skin is directly attached to its containment suit's interior" is one of the best I've read in that past couple of months in terms of sheer "what the fuck" level. On the other, it's yet another SCP that thinks because it has some cheaply done family drama in its backstory that I'm going to become invested and give a shit. Seriously, enough of those, please.

Upvote for now.
